heading: Congressional declaration of purpose; short title
label: 390aa Congressional declaration of purpose; short title
text: This subchapter shall amend and supplement the Act of , and Acts supplementary thereto and amendatory thereof (), hereinafter referred to as “Federal reclamation law”. This subchapter may be referred to as the “Reclamation Reform Act of 1982”.June 17, 190243 U.S.C. 371
notes: References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this title”, meaning title II (§§ 201–230) of , , , known as the Reclamation Reform Act of 1982, which enacted this subchapter, amended sections 373a, 422e, 425b, and 485h of this title, and repealed , Indians. For complete classification of title II to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 97–29396 Stat. 1263section 383 of Title 25Oct. 12, 1982Act of , referred to in text, is , , popularly known as the Reclamation Act, which is classified generally to this chapter.
